accurate.why: All claims align with the bill text and accurate context like the FTC and Protecting Children in the 21st Century Act amendment.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; the FTC enforcement and statute amended are correctly identified.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies the bill's purpose of establishing federal requirements to protect minors online.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Notes the amendment to the Protecting Children in the 21st Century Act creating an FTC-led education campaign.

exceptions_conditions.why: Captures key carve-outs like self-attestation insufficiency, no government ID requirement, research exceptions, and no age-gating requirement.

effective_dates.why: Accurately notes the one-year, 180-day, 90-day, and 18-month timing provisions.

major_provisions_covered.why: Covers all six titles' essential operative effects faithfully.

objective_tone.why: Neutral, descriptive tone without advocacy or editorializing.

coherent.why: Well-organized and internally consistent, following the bill's title structure.

concise.why: Compact with no padding or repetition.

correct_entities.why: FTC, state attorneys general, and the amended statute are all correctly identified.

correct_figures.why: Figures such as one-third content threshold, 180 days, 90 days, 18 months, and 3-hour break are all correct.
